skill.name: receiving-code-review
skill.description: Guides agents through code review feedback with verification, clarification, reasoned pushback, and careful implementation.
skill.summary: Evaluate code review feedback before changing code.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer command and crypto findings are false positives from Markdown fences, prose, and emoji-marked examples. The skill is documentation-only, but it does recommend a GitHub CLI API command for replying to review comments, which justifies a medium warning for external command and network use.

medium_findings.title: GitHub CLI API Command Suggested
medium_findings.description: The skill tells agents to reply to GitHub review comments with gh api. This is legitimate for pull request workflow, but it can make authenticated network requests and post comments if executed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is explicit and semantically relevant to the skill. It is not malicious, but it does require GitHub credentials and network access if run.

low_findings.title: Markdown Fences Misclassified As Ruby Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Markdown code fences as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These are prose examples and workflow blocks, not executable skill code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Every listed location is a Markdown fence around natural-language examples or pseudocode. No Ruby code or shell backtick execution appears in those locations.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Pattern Is Prose Substring Match
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography at prose lines, but the skill does not implement encryption or request cryptographic operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are descriptive text and a response example. No cryptographic API, algorithm selection, or security implementation is present.

low_findings.title: Network Reconnaissance Pattern Not Supported By Context
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged network reconnaissance at a line about using technical reasoning. No port scan, host probing, or discovery command is present there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact line is a communication guideline, not a command or networking instruction. I found no evidence of reconnaissance behavior in that location.

dangerous_patterns.title: Authenticated External Command
dangerous_patterns.description: The gh api command can modify GitHub discussion state through an authenticated API call. Users should review target repository, pull request, and comment identifiers before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is explicit and operational. The command is expected for this workflow, but it still performs a network action with user credentials.

content.value_statement: Code review feedback can be unclear, incomplete, or technically wrong for the current codebase. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code verify suggestions before implementing them.
actual_capabilities: Structures review feedback into read, understand, verify, evaluate, respond, and implement steps.
Prompts agents to ask clarifying questions before partial implementation.
Encourages technical verification against existing code and tests.
Provides guidance for pushing back on incorrect or unnecessary suggestions.
Defines an order for handling multi-item review feedback.
Advises GitHub inline reply handling for pull request comment threads.
limitations: Does not inspect code or run tests by itself.
Does not decide product or architecture tradeoffs without user direction.
May recommend GitHub CLI usage that requires valid local credentials.
Does not replace human judgment for sensitive or high-impact changes.

use_cases.title: Review Feedback Triage
use_cases.description: Sort unclear, blocking, simple, and complex review comments before changing code.

use_cases.title: Agent Pair Programming
use_cases.description: Guide an AI coding agent to verify reviewer suggestions before applying patches.

use_cases.title: Pull Request Response Discipline
use_cases.description: Respond to correct and incorrect feedback with concise technical reasoning.

prompt_templates.title: Clarify Review Feedback
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to read the review comments and identify anything unclear before making changes.

prompt_templates.title: Verify A Reviewer Suggestion
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to check whether this review suggestion is correct for the current codebase before implementing it.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Multi-Item Fixes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to order these review items by risk, dependency, and test impact before editing files.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Technical Pushback
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to draft concise technical pushback for review feedback that appears incorrect or unnecessary.

output_examples.input: A reviewer asks to remove legacy compatibility code.
output_examples.output: The response verifies the supported platform range first, then either removes the code or explains why compatibility still requires it.

output_examples.input: A reviewer says to fix six items, but two are unclear.
output_examples.output: The response states which items are understood and asks for clarification before starting implementation.

output_examples.input: A reviewer asks for a full metrics feature that may be unused.
output_examples.output: The response checks actual usage and asks whether to remove the unused endpoint instead of expanding it.

best_practices: Restate unclear requirements before editing code.
Verify each suggestion against the codebase, tests, and product context.
Implement one review item at a time and test each change.
anti_patterns: Do not accept review feedback before checking technical correctness.
Do not batch unclear items with clear items.
Do not add unused features only because a reviewer requested a more complete implementation.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps an agent evaluate, clarify, and implement code review feedback with technical discipline.

faq.question: Does it automatically change code?
faq.answer: No. It provides a workflow for deciding what to change and when to ask questions.

faq.question: Can it push back on reviewer comments?
faq.answer: Yes. It gives criteria for concise technical pushback when feedback is wrong or lacks context.

faq.question: Does it support GitHub review threads?
faq.answer: Yes. It advises replying inside inline GitHub review threads instead of posting top-level comments.

faq.question: Is network access required?
faq.answer: Only the GitHub CLI reply guidance uses network access, and only if the user chooses to run it.

faq.question: Who should use this skill?
faq.answer: Developers and AI coding agents handling pull request feedback, review comments, or external suggestions.
